description_long: This object consists of four large knives with broad, curved blades and wooden handles, arranged in a stacked, parallel formation. The blades are dark, possibly made of blackened or high-carbon steel, and each has a distinct curvature reminiscent of a kukri or machete style, with a single sharpened edge. The handles are cylindrical, made of polished wood with brass or bronze end caps and collars, giving them a robust and traditional appearance. The blades show some subtle patterning or discoloration, possibly from forging or use, and the handles are smooth with a slight ergonomic contour. There are no visible guards between the blade and handle, and the knives are not sheathed. The overall look is utilitarian yet refined, suitable for heavy-duty cutting or chopping tasks.
description: A set of four large, curved-blade knives with dark metal blades and polished wooden handles, arranged in parallel. The knives feature brass accents and a robust, traditional design.
description_view_0: The knives are viewed from the handle end, showing the rounded wooden pommels and brass end caps stacked vertically.
description_view_1: The full side profile of the knives is visible, highlighting the broad, curved blades and the smooth wooden handles with brass accents.
description_view_2: The knives are seen from the blade tip, showing the thinness of the blades and the alignment of the handles in a straight line.
description_view_3: Another side profile similar to view 1, but from the opposite side, again displaying the curvature of the blades and the wooden handles.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, polished wood with brass end caps and collars, smooth ergonomic contour
